documents.text: Chennai Super Kings - Wikipedia In the IPL, it has appeared in a 10 finals and qualified for the playoff stages 12 times (including consecutive appearances in their first 10 seasons), the ... The Super Kings is the joint-most successful IPL franchise, having won five IPL titles (along with Mumbai Indians). In the IPL, it has appeared in a 10 finals and qualified for the playoff stages 12 times (including consecutive appearances in their first 10 seasons), the most amongst the IPL teams. The franchise has also won the Champions League Twenty20 twice in 2010 and 2014. The team is currently captained by Ruturaj Gaikwad and coached by Stephen Fleming. The Super Kings was suspended for two years from the IPL starting July 2015 due to the involvement of its owners in the 2013 IPL betting case. The frachise re-joined the IPL for the 2018 season and won the title in its comeback season. In January 2022, CSK became India's first unicorn sports enterprise. As of 2022, it was the second most valuable IPL franchise with a valuation of $1.15 billion. Championship and further success (2010–12) In 2010, the Super Kings struggled in the first half of the regular season, winning only two matches out of seven.[19] They won five of their next seven games including a must-win encounter against Kings XI Punjab at Dharamshala.[20] With seven wins from 14 matches, the team finished the league stage in third place and qualified for the semi-finals for the third consecutive season.[20][21] In the semifinal, the Super Kings scored a modest 142/7 in 20 overs against the defending champions Deccan Chargers but restricted the opposition to 104 runs to score a 38-run victory.[22] The Super Kings defeated Mumbai Indians by 22 runs in the final to secure their first ever IPL title.[23][24] With the win, the franchise qualified for the 2010 Champions League Twenty20 that was to be held in South Africa. After not playing the 2016 and 2017 editions of the IPL, Chennai Super Kings, in 2018, proved that T20 cricket was not just the young man’s game. The Indian Premier League is the premier domestic T20 competition in India and has been organised by the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) since 2008. The IPL currently features 10 cricket teams and Kolkata Knight Riders are the defending champions. Having won the trophy on five occasions - 2010, 2011, 2018, 2021 and 2023 - under MS Dhoni, Chennai Super Kings are the joint-most successful team in the competition alongside Mumbai Indians. CSK have also finished runners-up on five different occasions. No team has made the final of the IPL on more occasions. Mumbai Indians won the IPL in 2013, 2015, 2019, 2019 and 2020 whilst finishing runners-up once in 2010. Rohit Sharma led MI to all of their five titles, which makes him the joint-most successful captain in the IPL alongside Dhoni. The inaugural edition of IPL was won by Rajasthan Royals in 2008. RR, led by Australian legend Shane Warne, defeated Chennai Super Kings in the final to take the crown. Kolkata Knight Riders are the only team besides CSK and MI to win multiple IPL titles. Led by Gautam Gambhir, KKR won the IPL in 2012 and 2014 while Shreyas Iyer orchestrated their third title win in 2024. They also finished runners-up in 2021, losing to CSK. Gujarat Titans entered the IPL in 2022 and won the title in their debut season before finishing as runners-up in 2023. Under Aussie opener David Warner, Sunrisers Hyderabad won the IPL title in 2016, beating Royal Challengers Bangalore in the final. The now-defunct Deccan Chargers are the only other team to win the 2009 iteration of IPL played in South Africa.

Gujarat Titans, Sunrisers Hyderabad, ... Up until 2021, the IPL tournament involved each team playing every other team twice in a home-and-away, double round-robin format.[13][14] From 2022, the ten teams were divided into two groups of five. A random draw was used to determine the groups and who plays whom across the groups once and twice. In the group stage, each team plays 14 games facing the other four teams in their group two times each (one home and one away game), four teams in the other group once, and the remaining team two times.[15] At the conclusion of the double round-robin league, on the basis of aggregate points, the top four teams qualify for the playoffs.[16][17] In this stage, the top two teams compete with each other (in a match titled "Qualifier 1"), as do the remaining two teams (in a match titled "Eliminator").[18] While the winner of Qualifier 1 directly qualifies for the final match, the losing team gets another chance to qualify for the final match by playing the winning team of the Eliminator match; this match is titled Qualifier 2.[13][18] The winner of this subsequent Qualifier 2 match moves onto the final match.[18] The team that wins the final match is crowned the Indian Premier League champion.[18]

Chennai Super Kings IPL History: How many times has CSK won ... Chennai Super Kings (CSK) are one of IPL's most successful teams, winning the tournament five times, with their latest victory in 2023. Led by the legendary MS Dhoni, CSK's journey in the IPL has been nothing short of remarkable, marked by triumphs and challenges. Let's take a closer look at how many times CSK has won the IPL and their journey to victory in each season. IPL 2011: Champions CSK continued their dominance in the IPL by winning back-to-back titles in 2011. Murali Vijay's explosive innings of 95 powered CSK to a massive total, setting up a comfortable victory over Royal Challengers Bangalore in the final. IPL 2018: Champions Live Events After facing a two-year ban, CSK made a stunning comeback in 2018, claiming their third IPL title. Shane Watson's heroic century in the final against Sunrisers Hyderabad played a crucial role in CSK's victory. IPL 2021: Champions CSK bounced back from a disappointing season in 2020 to clinch their fourth IPL title in 2021. Ruturaj Gaikwad, Faf du Plessis, and Josh Hazlewood were among the key performers for CSK throughout the season. IPL 2023: Champions In 2023, CSK added another feather to their cap by winning their fifth IPL title. Despite a rain-interrupted final against Gujarat Titans, CSK emerged victorious, with Devon Conway's crucial knock and MS Dhoni's leadership guiding them to glory. CSK's Legacy in the IPL CSK's success in the IPL can be attributed to their consistent performances, experienced squad, and strong leadership under MS Dhoni. Despite facing challenges and setbacks, CSK has always managed to bounce back stronger, showcasing their resilience and determination.
